parameters.content:
### When Chat Message Received

Opens a hosted chat session; every user message starts an execution with `chatInput` + `sessionId`.

Next: Frame the Assignment

### Frame the Assignment

Shapes the item: writes `assignment`, `deadline`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Coordinator Agent

### Coordinator Agent

The AI brain: reads the input, decides which attached tools to call (and in what order), then composes the answer.

Ports: â‘ â†’ Unpack Sections Â· â‘¡â†’ Capture Failure Context

âš  failures exit via the error rail

### Anthropic Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `languageModel`

ðŸ”‘ Anthropic API key

### Research Specialist (Sub-workflow)

Specialist sub-workflow exposed as a callable tool â€” one narrow capability per tool.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `tool`

### Draft Specialist (Sub-workflow)

Specialist sub-workflow exposed as a callable tool â€” one narrow capability per tool.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `tool`

### Enforce Result Schema

Forces the agent's answer into the JSON schema below â€” downstream nodes rely on this exact shape.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `outputParser`

### Unpack Sections

Explodes the `output.sections` array into one item per element.

Next: Persist Deliverable to Google Sheets

### Persist Deliverable to Google Sheets

Appends a new row to 'Conversation Tracker' â€” a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: â‘ â†’ Align Field Names Â· â‘¡â†’ Capture Failure Context

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record â€” searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Send Summary to Slack

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#support`) â€” a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

# Reply to chat messages for Lead Bots (07785)

**Pattern:** Chatbot Responder Â· **Level:** Core Â· **Trigger:** Chat

## What it does
Reply to chat messages for Lead Bots (07785). It responds live to chat messages.

## Why it exists
Manual lead bots is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
When Chat Message Received â†’ Frame the Assignment â†’ Coordinator Agent â†’ Unpack Sections â†’ Persist Deliverable to Google Sheets â†’ Align Field Names â†’ Save Execution Metadata â†’ â€¦

## Use it for
- Teams that want to chatbot responder without manual effort
- Anyone automating lead bots in customer support
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Has retries and error handling built in

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Anthropic API key; Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: ðŸŸ¦ entry Â· ðŸŸª AI Â· ðŸŸ« actions Â· â¬œ utilities Â· ðŸŸ© outputs Â· ðŸŸ¥ error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath â€” read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger â€” hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click â†’ Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The Coordinator Agent never does the specialist work itself â€” each specialist is delegated to its own sub-workflow via a Workflow Tool, so every specialist stays single-responsibility and testable in isolation instead of one giant prompt trying to do everything. An output parser forces the final answer into JSON so downstream nodes can rely on the shape instead of parsing free text.

## Practice exercises

Once it's wired up with real credentials, try:

1. Add a third specialist tool and update the system message to explain when the agent should reach for it.
2. Loosen or tighten the JSON schema on the output parser and see how the agent's final answer shape changes.
3. Replace one placeholder Workflow Tool with a real n8n sub-workflow and measure the round-trip latency.

## ðŸ›Ÿ Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3Ã— with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail â†’ context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## ðŸ¤– AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens â€” keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## ðŸ›  Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see ðŸ”‘ lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your lead bots process. Every node card explains its role â€” safe to extend at any marked branch.
